commit.message:
Apply 14 editorial changes (M.5–M.17) to Native AI PHY 6G survey

- M.5: Replace Table II analysis with block-length boundary discussion
- M.6: Add Section III.A.8 Rayleigh Channel Results (Table III)
- M.7: Fix figure numbering (Figure 5↔Figure 3 swap)
- M.8: Add 3GPP/ITU Coverage column to Table I
- M.9: Condense Section II (remove verbose equations, add cross-references)
- M.10: Add Proposition 1 with 4 properties for I_sem (refs [91],[92])
- M.12: Add citation [10] in VII.B short block-length finding
- M.13: Add LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS section before Section I
- M.14: Add [cf. 90] citation for LDPC ASIC; add refs [90]–[92],[94]–[97]
- M.15: Add Table V computational complexity comparison
- M.16: Replace verbose PyTorch code with condensed pseudocode
- M.17: Add Section VI.J (RIS with AI) and VI.K (NTN/LEO with AI)
- Update Article Organization and VII.A to mention VI.J and VI.K

Note: refs [51],[52] already in use; Carnap/Bao references use [91],[92]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix temporal reference in evaluation report scoring table

Update 'Actualidad' row label to 'hasta 2025-2026' for accuracy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add evaluation report for Native_AI_Physical_Layer_6G_IEEE.md

Comprehensive peer-review evaluation report in Spanish for the article
'Native Artificial Intelligence at the Physical Layer of 6G Networks:
Foundations, Architectures, and Perspectives' targeting IEEE Wireless
Communications (Q1, IF≈12.9).

Report covers 14 evaluation dimensions (A–N):
- Executive summary with Major Revision verdict
- Global scoring table (3.0/5)
- Critical deficiencies: 8/8 figures PENDING, empty references section
- Topic relevance (5/5): 6G, AI-native PHY, Foundation/Diffusion Models
- Mathematical rigor analysis (4/5) with specific error identification
- Table II benchmark evaluation (autoencoder vs. Turbo/LDPC/Polar)
- Venue recommendation: IEEE COMST preferred over IEEE Wireless Comm.
- 17 specific improvement recommendations (5 critical, 8 important, 4 minor)
- Estimated 4-6 week revision timeline

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: scaffold HashUtopia v3.0 React application

- React 18 + TypeScript + Vite project setup
- Tailwind CSS v4 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin
- Zustand stores: auth, ui, mining state management
- @tanstack/react-query for server state
- Simulated WebSocket with demo event streaming
- Atomic design: atoms/molecules/organisms/templates
- Feature pages: Dashboard, Mining, Trading, Wallet, Admin
- DOMPurify XSS protection in AdminTicketManager rich text editor
- Hash units: BTC=TH/s, ETC=MH/s, XMR=kH/s, LTC=MH/s
- React Router v7 with lazy-loaded routes and auth guards
- Footer z-50 isolation fix with AI demo section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: marcusjenkinscode <264086284+marcusjenkinscode@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rename project from Dwiggy to Hunto globally

Updated all references across:
- Backend: config.go, README.md, docker-compose.yml
- Mobile: app.json, package.json, apiClient.js
- Admin: package.json
- Root: README.md

Changes:
- Database name: dwiggy → hunto
- Bundle IDs: com.dwiggy.app → com.hunto.app
- Package names: dwiggy-* → hunto-*
- Storage bucket: dwiggy-images → hunto-images
- Token keys: dwiggy_* → hunto_*

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add English translation of sections IV and V (section_4_5_en.md)

Translate Section IV (End-to-End System Architectures) and Section V
(Practical Implementation and Computational Complexity) from Spanish to
English for IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials submission.

- All LaTeX expressions preserved exactly as-is
- All reference numbers preserved
- Python comments translated; variable names unchanged
- Architecture diagram labels translated
- FIGURA_PENDIENTE → FIGURE_PENDING with figure descriptions in English
- Technical IEEE terminology maintained

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add English translation of Section III: Physical Layer Components with Native AI

Translate section_3.md from Spanish to English for IEEE Communications
Surveys & Tutorials (Q1) submission. Translation covers:
- III.A: Neural Channel Coding (autoencoders, RNN decoders, performance
  analysis, Table II benchmark results)
- III.B: Channel Estimation with Deep Learning (CNN/U-Net, BiLSTM,
  physics-informed NNs, massive MIMO compression)
- III.C: Multi-User Signal Detection (DetNet, LISTA, algorithm unrolling, RL)
- III.D: Intelligent Beamforming (black-box NN, WMMSE unrolling,
  multi-agent RL, hardware constraints, predictive beamforming)
- III.E: Radio Resource Management with AI (DQN scheduling, GCN, game theory)

All LaTeX math expressions, reference numbers, and English technical terms
preserved exactly. FIGURA_PENDIENTE placeholders converted to FIGURE_PENDING
with figure descriptions translated to English.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Restructure Sec VII, update I.D, add II reader note, expand VII.A contributions

- Task 1 (VII.C): Add cross-reference note to Sec VI.I (3GPP Release 18/19, ITU-R IMT-2030) at start of Roadmap Tecnológico
- Task 2 (I.D): Expand Section VI description to include new subsections VI.G, VI.H, VI.I (2024–2025 developments)
- Task 3 (II intro): Add specialized-reader navigation note after Section II header, directing to Sec II.D for domain-specific formalism
- Task 4 (VII.A): Add contributions #6 (Foundation/Diffusion Models, 3GPP) and #7 (Reproducible BER Benchmark) to Síntesis de Contribuciones

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add original BER benchmark subsection III.A.7 and update Sec. VII.B references

- Add new subsection III.A.7: 'Benchmark de Referencia: Autoencoder vs. Códigos Convencionales en Bloques Cortos'
  - Reproducible experimental setup: AWGN channel, n∈{7,16,32,64}, rate 1/2
  - Compares autoencoder MLP vs. Turbo, LDPC, Polar, and PPV finite-blocklength bound
  - Tabla II with Es/N0 required for BER=10^-3 across all schemes and block lengths
  - Analysis of autoencoder advantage (0.3–1.4 dB over Polar for n≤32)
  - Reproducibility and limitations notes
- Update Sec. VII.B to cite Sec. III.A.7 and Tabla II with specific quantitative references

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 8 figure placeholders to 6G AI-native article

Insert FIGURA_PENDIENTE blocks with detailed descriptions for future
figure generation at appropriate locations throughout the article:

- Figura 1 (after Section I.D): Architectural comparison Traditional vs
  AI-Native (End-to-End Autoencoder)
- Figura 2 (beginning of Section III): Taxonomy of AI techniques for 6G PHY
- Figura 3 (after autoencoder training code in Section IV.A): MIMO
  communication autoencoder architecture with differentiable channel
- Figura 4 (Section III.B.2, after U-Net description): U-Net architecture
  for OFDM channel estimation
- Figura 5 (Section III.A.4, after Shannon gap discussion): BER vs Eb/N0
  comparison curves for neural codes vs Turbo/LDPC/Polar
- Figura 6 (Section IV.B.4, after JSCC results table): JSCC neural
  architecture and PSNR vs SNR curves
- Figura 7 (Section IV.D.2, after ISAC multi-task block): ISAC system
  architecture with dual-function waveform processing
- Figura 8 (Section VII.C, before roadmap phases): Technological roadmap
  2024-2035 for AI-native 6G PHY

Each placeholder includes in-text reference in the surrounding paragraph.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add subsections VI.G, VI.H, VI.I: Foundation Models, Diffusion Models, 3GPP IA normative

- VI.G: Foundation Models y LLMs para PHY (2024-2025): paradigma general FM,
  aplicaciones en estimación de canal universal, gestión de recursos, diseño
  asistido, ChannelFormer para OFDM, y desafíos (latencia, dominio, energía).
- VI.H: Modelos de Difusión para estimación de canal y generación de datos:
  proceso forward/reverse, formulación MMSE-posterior, datos sintéticos,
  y limitaciones de latencia DDIM/consistency models.
- VI.I: Arquitecturas 3GPP Release 18/19: TR 38.843, TR 38.859, TS 28.540,
  arquitectura AIMLF, y requisitos ITU-R IMT-2030 (M.2160-0) para IA nativa.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Resolve remaining [Ref] placeholder for Qin et al. [64] in comparison table

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix reference issues in Native AI 6G article

- Task 1: Remove duplicate refs [13]=[5], [42]=[27], [44]=[9]; update body citations [13]→[5], [42]→[27], [44]→[9]
- Task 2: Fix misassigned citations: [3]→[1],[2] and [4]→[2] in Sec I.A; [11]→[17] for Shannon separation theorem; [12]→[14] for practical 6G scenarios
- Task 3: Update [75] ONNX reference to proper IEEE online format
- Task 4: Add new references [79]–[89]
- Task 5: Add [79],[80],[89] in Sec VI.D; [82] in semantic comms; resolve [Ref] placeholders in table with [85],[86],[87],[82]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix mathematical errors in Native AI 6G article

- FIX 1: Complete truncated WMMSE Receive Combining formula (Section III.D.3)
- FIX 2: Clarify MIMO water-filling notation with explicit lambda_i definition (Section II.B.1)
- FIX 3: Expand Semantic Mutual Information definition with formal non-circular formulation citing Carnap & Bar-Hillel (1952) and Xie et al. (2021) (Section IV.C.4)
- FIX 4: Correct typo 'Se demostr   que' → 'Se demostró que' (Section VII.A)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Revise Abstract, Keywords, and Section I of Native AI 6G article

- CHANGE 1: Reframe abstract as a comprehensive survey with quantitative
  scope indicators (~78 refs, 2017-2025, 5 PHY components, 8 AI families)
- CHANGE 2: Replace Section I.C contributions with 6 survey dimensions,
  adding Dimension VI on literature comparison and BER benchmark
- CHANGE 3: Add Tabla I (literature comparison table) in Section I.C
  positioning this survey against Qin, Letaief, Chen, Gündüz, Zhu et al.
- CHANGE 4: Extend Palabras clave with Survey Comprehensivo, Foundation
  Models para Comunicaciones, Modelos de Difusión, 3GPP Release 18/19

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Section 4: detailed improvement recommendations for IEEE Q1 publication

- Add Section 4 (RECOMENDACIONES DE MEJORA PARA PUBLICACIÓN IEEE Q1) to evaluation report
- Section A: 4 critical changes (language, article type, figures, bibliography)
- Section B: 4 major changes (standards update, length, COMST coverage, experimental validation)
- Section C: 5 minor changes (typos, incomplete formula, notation consistency)
- Section D: action plan table with 13 items (problem/severity/action/section/effort)
- Section E: editorial verdict with COMST as first option (IF ~35.6), JSAC as second (IF ~16.4)
- Estimated revision timeline: 10-14 weeks for COMST, 8-12 weeks for JSAC

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Sección 1: Evaluación de Pertinencia Temática, Originalidad y Actualidad

- Pertinencia y relevancia del tema (alta y no cuestionable)
- Análisis de actualidad referenciando 3GPP TR 38.843, TR 38.859 e ITU-R IMT-2030
- Evaluación de originalidad y tipología como survey comprehensivo
- Tabla comparativa de contribuciones vs. literatura existente (6 trabajos)
- Lagunas temáticas: LLMs/foundation models, diffusion models, 3GPP normativos, green AI
- Impacto potencial en IEEE COMST o IEEE JSAC

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: corregir número ITU-R IMT-2030 y añadir ejemplo formato IEEE para ref. web ONNX

- Clarifica que M.2160 (2015) corresponde a IMT-2020, no a IMT-2030;
  indica que el autor debe verificar el número definitivo asignado en AR-23
- Añade ejemplo concreto de formato IEEE correcto para recurso web institucional (ONNX)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Sección 3: Evaluación de Referencias y Bibliografía to informe IEEE Q1 6G PHY

- Análisis general: 78 refs, distribución temporal 2017-2023, sesgos identificados
- Tabla de 4 casos de duplicación con acciones correctivas por par
- 4 referencias mal asignadas en contexto (Shannon 1948, Cover & Thomas, Kim ICLR 2018, Aoudia 2018)
- 7 lagunas bibliográficas críticas (3GPP TR 38.843, ITU-R M.2160, Gündüz 2022, etc.)
- Problemas de formato: capitalización, ref. [75] ONNX, autores corporativos
- 5 recomendaciones de corrección priorizadas

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Mejorar formato LaTeX de ecuaciones en evaluación 6G PHY

Convertir fórmulas complejas a modo display (línea independiente) para
mejorar renderizado en distintos procesadores Markdown/LaTeX.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: eastaizah <258072108+eastaizah@users.noreply.github.com>

Añadir evaluación técnica y matemática del artículo 6G PHY AI-Nativo

Redacción de la sección 'Evaluación de Contenido Técnico y Rigor Matemático'
del informe de revisión IEEE Q1 para el artículo 'Inteligencia Artificial
Nativa en el Nivel Físico de Redes 6G'. Incluye:
- Clasificación tipológica (survey vs. investigación original)
- Evaluación de rigor matemático con fortalezas y deficiencias específicas
- Errores identificados por sección/ecuación (WMMSE Sec. III.D, I_sem Sec. IV.C.4)
- Análisis de referencias duplicadas y mal asignadas
- Evaluación de validación experimental ausente

commit.message:
Implement GitHub App manifest flow for webhook integration (#123)

* feat: add GitHub App integration for automatic webhook delivery

Replace manual webhook URL/secret configuration with GitHub App-based
auth. Users install the GitHub App once, and webhooks are delivered
automatically. Codespaces linked to GitHub repos get event sources
and subscriptions auto-configured.

New files:
- GitHubAppService: installation management, auto-configure events
- GitHub App webhook route: /hooks/github-app (signature-verified)
- GitHub App API routes: /api/github/app/* (status, installations)

Schema changes:
- github_installations: add teamId column
- event_sources: add githubInstallationId FK

Frontend: Settings > GitHub now shows "Install GitHub App" flow
instead of manual PAT configuration.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EF9YD956jQ7ouvj6EYku2y

* fix: address review findings - Postgres schema parity, cascade policy, error handling

- Add teamId to Postgres github_installations (was only in SQLite)
- Add githubInstallationId to Postgres event_sources (was only in SQLite)
- Change github_installations.teamId onDelete from cascade to set null
  (installations are GitHub-side resources that outlive team membership)
- Add error handling to handleRemoveInstallation in frontend
- Fix getInstallationToken to use getAppOctokit directly (avoid
  double-token creation)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EF9YD956jQ7ouvj6EYku2y

* fix: address backend review - orphan cleanup, result checking, URL safety

- removeInstallation now cleans up event sources and codespace refs
  before deleting (prevents orphaned FKs)
- Check handleInstallation/handleUninstall results in webhook handler
  (don't silently swallow failures)
- URL-encode GITHUB_APP_NAME in install URL for defense in depth

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EF9YD956jQ7ouvj6EYku2y

* feat: add GitHub App manifest flow for self-service app creation

Users can now create a GitHub App through the UI without manually
configuring environment variables. The manifest flow:

1. User enters their external URL and clicks "Create GitHub App"
2. Form POSTs manifest to GitHub for one-click app creation
3. GitHub redirects back with a code
4. Backend exchanges code for credentials (private key, webhook
   secret, etc.) and stores them encrypted in the settings table
5. User installs the app on their org (existing flow)

Changes:
- GitHubAppService: add getCredentials(), saveCredentials(),
  isConfiguredAsync() with DB + env var credential resolution
- getAppOctokit(): accept dynamic credentials, cache invalidation
- New routes: POST /manifest, POST /setup-callback, DELETE /credentials
- Webhook handler: load secret from DB when env var not set
- Frontend: three-state UI (Not Created → Created → Installed)
- Settings allowlist: github.app.credentials with encryption

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EF9YD956jQ7ouvj6EYku2y

* refactor: simplify GitHub App integration - remove env vars, add caching, deduplicate

- Remove env var fallback entirely — credentials are DB-only via
  manifest flow (no more GITHUB_APP_ID, GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY env vars)
- Add in-memory credential cache (invalidated on save/delete) to
  avoid repeated DB reads + decryption on every webhook
- Fix triple getCredentials() call in /status route — single call
- Extract buildInstallUrl() helper (was duplicated 4 times)
- Remove dead sync methods (getInstallUrl, isConfigured sync variant)
- Consolidate frontend state (3 useState → 1 AppStatus object)
- Use finally for loadData in error paths
- Parallelize independent DB writes in handleUninstall/removeInstallation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EF9YD956jQ7ouvj6EYku2y

* test: add 22 integration tests for GitHubAppService

Tests cover:
- Credential management: save, retrieve, encrypt, cache, invalidate, delete
- Installation lifecycle: create, upsert, uninstall, list, filter by team
- Auto-configure: event source + subscription creation for codespaces,
  reuse existing sources, idempotency
- Cleanup: removeInstallation cleans up event sources and codespaces
- Helper: buildInstallUrl

Also adds migration v23 (github_installations.team_id +
event_sources.github_installation_id) and updates test DB setup.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EF9YD956jQ7ouvj6EYku2y

* refactor: use existing utilities - errorMessage, validateIdParam, errorResponse

Replace hand-rolled patterns with existing codebase utilities:
- errorMessage() instead of `error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)`
- validateIdParam() instead of manual isValidId + error JSON
- errorResponse() instead of manual result.error destructuring

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EF9YD956jQ7ouvj6EYku2y

* fix: resolve 4 TypeScript errors causing CI failures

commit.message:
Complete Metal.jl GPU acceleration

- Fix run_gpu! loop to follow exact 16-step CPU integration order
- Fix recovery duration: use lognormal (matching CPU default) instead of exponential
- Fix dynamic network support: re-generate edges each step (was using static cached edges)
- Add SEIR GPU support (exposed→infected transition, recovery time setting)
- Fix SIS GPU: use ti_recovered-based recovery, add immunity waning + boost
- Add cache_edges option for static network optimization
- Optimize results: GPU-side sum() instead of full state download
- GPUDiseaseArrays extended with exposed/ti_exposed fields
- Add _sample_recovery_durations helper matching CPU distribution
- Correctness verified: r>0.999 for SIR, SIS, SEIR (30 seeds)
- Update GPU vignette with actual benchmarks and working examples
- Extension now 1,696 lines (was 1,385)

commit.message:
fix(android): render GIF as window background to eliminate shake

Moves giphy.gif out of the MAUI view hierarchy entirely and renders it
as the Android Activity window background using AnimatedImageDrawable
(API 28+). Animation frames now call invalidate() only on the Window
decor-view background drawable — completely below MAUI's compositing
layer — so MAUI layout is never touched during animation.

Changes:
- Resources/Images/giphy.gif → Resources/Raw/giphy.gif (MauiAsset)
  so it is accessible via Android Assets.Open() at runtime
- Platforms/Android/MainActivity.cs: override OnCreate, read GIF bytes,
  wrap in ByteBuffer, decode via ImageDecoder (API 28+ guard + try/catch
  fallback), set Alpha=127 (50% opacity), call Start(), set as
  Window background drawable
- Views/MainPage.xaml: remove AbsoluteLayout + Image GIF block;
  set ContentPage and root Grid BackgroundColor=Transparent so the
  native window background shows through; BoxView gradient overlay kept

Pre-API-28 devices fall back gracefully (dark background only).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update electron-builder linux desktop config and add linux cloudflared support

- Move linux.desktop properties under 'entry' key for electron-builder 26.8.1 compatibility
- Add linux-x64 platform to cloudflared download script
- Add linux extraResources for cloudflared binary

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add linux maintainer for deb package build

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: remove ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE from Copilot CLI subprocess env

In packaged builds, ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE leaks into the Copilot CLI
subprocess environment, causing the CLI to malfunction and its stdin
stream to be destroyed prematurely ('Cannot call write after a stream
was destroyed'). Claude and OpenCode adapters already clean this up;
apply the same fix to the Copilot adapter.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve copilot CLI binary from asar.unpacked in packaged builds

In packaged Electron builds, import.meta.resolve returns paths inside
app.asar. Electron's patched fs.existsSync reports these files exist,
but the OS cannot execute binaries from inside an asar archive. This
caused the Copilot CLI process to fail immediately on spawn, resulting
in 'Cannot call write after a stream was destroyed'.

Fix: convert app.asar paths to app.asar.unpacked paths, where
electron-builder extracts the copilot binaries via asarUnpack config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix all critical and high security/performance issues

- [CRITICAL] Fix path traversal vulnerability in ServeImage endpoint
  * Sanitize filename with Path.GetFileName()
  * Validate resolved path stays within uploads directory
  * Prevents arbitrary file read attacks

- [CRITICAL] Fix N+1 query problems (3 locations)
  * GetPosts: Added .Include(p => p.Likes) to prevent 20+ queries per request
  * GetUserPosts: Added .Include(p => p.Likes) before projection
  * Result: From 21 queries to 2 queries per page load

- [CRITICAL] Fix race condition in ToggleLike
  * Wrapped in explicit transaction to prevent duplicate likes
  * Ensures atomic check-and-add operation

- [HIGH] Fix config rebuild on every image request
  * Injected IConfiguration via dependency injection
  * Eliminates wasteful ConfigurationBuilder instantiation

- [HIGH] Fix file stream disposal
  * Changed from File.OpenRead() to Results.File() with proper lifecycle

- [HIGH] Add input validation for auth
  * Username: 3-50 chars, alphanumeric + underscore only
  * Password: minimum 8 characters
  * Prevents weak credentials

- [HIGH] Add timeout on file upload
  * Added 30-second CancellationToken to prevent DoS attacks
  * Cleans up incomplete files on timeout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Support Lark in the Feishu channel (#69)

* feat: support Lark in Feishu channel

Add platform selection to the existing Feishu channel, pass the selected SDK domain into the Lark SDK clients, improve startup readiness for long connections, and document the Feishu/Lark permission templates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR feedback for Feishu startup

Avoid unnecessary adapter restarts on unchanged config saves, suppress noisy WS info logging while still detecting readiness, and clean up the startup monitor on early failures to prevent dangling timeout/rejection state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: polish Lark platform copy

Use platform-aware runtime log labels for the shared Feishu/Lark adapter and align the Russian locale strings with the new platform selector copy.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use Lark log scope for Lark channel

Switch the shared Feishu/Lark channel logger scope dynamically so runtime log prefixes use lark when the configured platform is Lark, and thread that logger through the adapter helpers with test coverage.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address websocket startup review feedback

Refine the Feishu/Lark WS startup monitor to keep SDK diagnostics visible, add a weaker start-resolution fallback if the SDK log markers change, simplify config merging without reintroducing undefined overwrites, and make the channel name platform-aware with targeted unit coverage.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix critical security and logic bugs found in code review

Security Fixes:
- CRITICAL: Remove eval() for BUILD_CMD and TEST_CMD in patch_test.sh
  Replaced with bash -c to prevent command injection via env variables
- HIGH: Replace /tmp/build.log and /tmp/test.log with mktemp()
  Prevents race conditions and data corruption in multi-user systems

Logic & Validation Fixes:
- HIGH: Fix division by zero vulnerability in patch_check.sh calc_similarity()
  Added proper handling for zero adds/dels cases
- MEDIUM: Add input validation for silicon test results in patch_test.sh
  Only accepts PASS/FAIL/PENDING, defaults to PENDING on invalid input
- MEDIUM: Fix per-file diff stat calculation in patch_check.sh
  Use awk to track adds/dels per file instead of counting entire patch
- MEDIUM: Add validation for empty review branch in patch_check.sh
  Dies with clear error if branch name cannot be extracted from JSON
- LINUX-ONLY: Simplified stat command to use only Linux syntax (macOS support deferred)

All Python tests still pass (29/29 ✅)
All fixes tested for correctness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: external-patch-handling
repository.description: Python CLI pipeline for structured cross-company BIOS patch exchange (BHS). Covers receive, apply, functional equivalence check, test, review report, and integration.

commit.message:
Add bash versions of patch pipeline scripts

- patch_receive.sh: Validate and stage patches from a directory
- patch_apply.sh: Apply staged patches to a review branch
- patch_check.sh: Compare sender intent vs actual changes landed
- patch_test.sh: Run build and unit tests, collect silicon results
- patch_integrate.sh: Cherry-pick reviewed patches to working branch

All bash scripts:
- Provide zero-dependency alternatives to Python versions
- Generate compatible JSON output for downstream steps
- Support same configuration via .patch-pipeline.toml and env vars
- Can be mixed with Python scripts in the same pipeline
- Include comprehensive error handling and user-friendly output

Added BASH_README.md with detailed usage guide and examples.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
